title: Lovely Light Korean Tofu Ssam
description: A funky and crisp Korean dish from Busan, perfect for a sick day, featuring ground cumin and pickle, and is vegetarian-friendly, served in the form of ssam.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the crumbled tofu and cook until lightly browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the ground cumin and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the chopped pickle and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Season the tofu mixture with soy sauce to taste.
To assemble the ssam, place a spoonful of the tofu mixture onto a perilla leaf, followed by a spoonful of cooked rice.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve immediately.
Serve with additional soy sauce and sesame oil on the side, if desired.
